About: Looking to be a CS major. Had various leadership positions and represented students. Is a nailtech. Python, JAVA, communication, financial skills. Is very creative too. Singing, baking, reading, cooking. Did research projects (physics passed at GOV School).

Eye-to-eye:

She was in a lab group with other people and one student didn’t pull his weight during the duration of the year. She had to sit this person down and discuss the situation. Was able to compromise with this individual and everything got figured out. There was change. 

Biggest weakness:

Inability to accept feeling behind. During Gov School she didn’t think that had to study, but was humbled really quickly. She had issues reaching out to people to help, but coming to tech is starting to open her up and she is actively looking for new friends. Takes a lot of initiative. 

I really really like this girl. We need her on infinitum. WE NEED HER ON INFINITUM.

Why Infinitum:
Even tho a CS major, she wants to improve her coding skills outside of the classroom. She wants to learn “in the field” with her teammates and wants to learn as much as possible.

column11: I liked Parker! He definitely showed he was passionate and framed a lot of his experiences well. He doesn't have the most technical experience but I think that's perfect. He seems to want to get involved with a hands on design team. He seems to have a good attitude towards loss, mentioned that he ran for a position in high school and that he lost to another candidate whom he respected and felt deserved his position. Interested in doing aero or meche because he likes to "tinker." He said he wanted to help people, asked him if he was interested in Juvo and he seemed somewhat excited about it as well so I put it as third choice. His dream is to work for NASA. He told me some really interested info about how he's taking a space mining class and learned about the possibility of harnessing helium 3 to support the energy crisis on earth. He was superrr passionate about that and I found it super interesting. For the eye-to-eye question, he said that him and a classmate working on a physics project had two different ideas of equations they wanted to use and decided to try both of theirs. Turned out, both of their ideas worked. He said they also discussed their ideas and both saw the validity in the other's. For the weakness question, he said he had poor time management for a time in high school where he'd go on his phone for 6 hours and go to bed at 3am (relatable). He said he decided to change that by locking his phone away every time he got home and he has not let up on that habit since. He says he still does it here, at the library, which is commendable. He has minimal CAD experience from high school. Didn't have many STEM opportunities at his school and is very interested in joining Archimedes now.

He did model UN for 5 years. Did crisis committee for UN. He is very into art and does personal projects. Likes to tell stories, and worldbuilding. Plays DND. Joined engineering because he likes hands-on projects. “After each day I can see the progress being made after I get home.” Likes to see the physical product of things.

Very busy junion/senior year. Did sports and a lot of clubs. Went to an IB school so he took a lot of difficult classes. Has an IB diploma. Secretary for Model UN for an honor society. He understood that he had to drop some commitments in order to put his education first. Was unable to continue with sports towards the end of his high school career in order to graduate with top marks. 
Eye-to-eye:
For Model UN, he started his own conference and was in charge of his own committee. His secretary was telling him that he couldn’t have internet in the room where his committee was based out of and made it very difficult to research information for his conference. He compromised with his secretary and was able to get a low-level internet connection for a few people in order to more efficiently work on his committee. 
Biggest Weakness:
He says that he is too much of a pushover (not evident by his group interview). He is a people pleaser, so much so that he doesn’t argue for his position on topics hes collaborating on. He says he’s trying to fix it.

column11: Very interested in joining Astra. He greeted me by name and I asked him how he knew my name, he said he knows all of our names! He did 3 years of VESS in high school over the summers, he's definitely very interested in NASA and NASA based programs. He was wearing a NASA cap. He's planning on double majoring in ECE. I asked him if he would be okay with a non ECE related Astra project and he said yes. He recently started to learn KiCAD for autoboat. I asked him about his time commitment and he said it won't be a problem, he has blocked out time specifically for archimedes. For the eye-to-eye question, he said that his VESS team had conflicting thought on whether to work on instrumentation or risk mitigation. He encouraged the team to work on risk mitigation first and saw that it actually made it easier for them to figure out instrumentation because they tackled the smaller issues first. For the failure question, he seemed more prepared to talk about obstacles than a failure. He talked about making a squirrel repellent device at home while in high school. His family and his neighbors had issues with squirrels getting into the garden so he made a device with Arduino to make a buzzing sound to scare them off. Said it didn't work well so he had to keep iterating. He asked some good questions about the Astra process and seemed to be good at proposal writing, which is reflected by his writing for the application. He has CAD and programming experience.

Hasn’t joined other clubs because his main project already takes 10–15 hours a week.
Working on finding balance and learning when “good enough” is enough.

He said he had to balance his priorities between eagle scout with school and college essays. Didn't elaborate too much, wasn't a super strong response. His project was to build wooden stairs for a community space. His response to the eye-to-eye question was that his colleague wanted the kids to keep playing whereas he felt they should take a break. He didn't elaborate much on how they decided so I asked and he said they talked it out and quickly resolved it, recognized each others points. I asked him about facing obstacles and he said it was difficult to organize logistics for his senior trip over the summer, he said he just tried to focus on the end goal. I asked him how he functions in a team, he said he can take lead when necessary and step back when he has to. I asked him if he was interested in working on anything in particular for Infinitum, he didn't have something specific in mind (which is fine) but said he's had ideas for social media apps before. I asked him more about his work for his internship, he confirmed that it was a lot of new information and that he worked on UI/UX for a statistics feature as well as some back end stuff.

Bowling team

Piano

column19:
Took a class in high school that was project based. Learned robotics and built robotics. She wants a job where she can be hands-on. Joined a personal project with her professor to build a custom dev-board.

Learning something new: moving from introversion to extrovert by being a girl scout. Example provided was when she was forced to talk to people by selling cookies and other scouting events. She talked to peers who are closer to her age that can guide her through that process. She tried to follow the standard 'cookie-selling' procedure out the window when she felt like it wasn't working. After talking with her troop leader, she changed the pitch by learning about the person first to make it more personal.

She had an internship for her school system through the Mayor's Program in Texas. She had a fellow intern that wasn't really doing anything, forcing all the work on her. After talking with the intern, they couldn't see eye-to-eye because the fellow intern didn't want to take it so seriously. Her manager then told her to talk it over. She resolved this by actually getting to know the person outside of work and helping to shape the workflow more to her style.

role in a group setting: She says her biggest weakness is trusting others to do their own work and that she is a bit of a perfectionist. Basically, an organizational lead.

failure recovery: she talked about how her coworker failed to call in an asthma attack during lifeguarding. She argued with the coworker about the seriousness of this. Lowkey didn't really answer the question.

Girl Scouts Gold Award: project in Bali. She contracted a disease in Bali and got deathly sick, making her realize the severity of the problem in this country. She sent out a brochure to residents warning them about the microplastics in the water, especially for new residents. She then got connected to a company that produces filtration products to clean sources for residents.

How do you manage priorities: She says it's hard for her to turn down friends not to go out. Google Calendar to manage activities.
